semanticCore: Stagnating, changing smell/taste, spoiling water, or losing fresh quality

keyDerivations.meaning: Stagnant, spoiled, altered in smell or taste by long standing

figurativeOrRareUsages: فِيهَا أَنْهَارٌ مِنْ مَاءٍ غَيْرِ آسِنٍ (47:15): Therein (in Paradise) are rivers of water never altering/stagnating (always fresh).
summaryEn: The root represents water becoming foul or stale due to stagnation (Asn), describing the rivers of Paradise as carrying perfectly pure, fresh water that never spoils.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر تغير ريح الماء وطعمه وفساده بسبب طول ركوده ودون سريان (اسن)، واصفاً أنهار مياه الجنة بالسلامة من هذا الأثر (غير آسن).
rawText: أَسَنَ 1 , aor. ﹹ (S, M, Mgh, Msb, K) and ﹻ, (S, M, K,) inf. n. أُسُونٌ (S, M, Msb) and أَسْنٌ; (M;) and أَسِنَ, aor. ﹷ, (S, M, &c.,) inf. n. أَسَنٌ; (S, M, Msb;) said of water, i. q. أَجَنَ and أَجِنَ; (S, K;) [i. e.] It became altered for the worse (M, Mgh, Msb) in odour, (M,) [or in taste and colour, from some such cause as long standing, (see أَجَنَ,)] but was drinkable; (M;) or so as not to be drunk, (Msb, TA,) thus differing from أَجَنَ and أَجِنَ. (TA.) [See also أَصِلَ.] أَسِنٌ : see what follows. آسِنٌ (S, Mgh, Msb, K) and 🡻أَسِنٌ, (S, Mgh, Msb,) applied to water, (S, Mgh, &c.,) i. q. آجِنٌ [and أَجِنٌ]; (S, K;) [i. e.] Altered for the worse (Mgh, Msb) in odour, (Mgh,) [or in taste and colour, from some such cause as long standing but drinkable; (see above, and see آجِنٌ;)] or so as not to be drunk, (Msb, TA,) thus differing from آجِنٌ and أَجِنٌ: (TA:) pl. [of the former] آسَانٌ [like as أَطْهَارٌ is pl. of طَاهِرٌ, or perhaps it may have for its sing. أَسْنٌ, like أَجْنٌ]. (M, TA.) مَنْ مَآءٍ غَيْرِ آسِنٍ, in the Kur [xlvii. 16], is explained by Fr as meaning Of water not altered for the worse; not آجِن. (TA.)
